title: papaver alpinum subsp. flaviflorum
intro: Papaver alpinum subsp. flaviflorum, also known as the yellow alpine poppy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and delicate, papery petals. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Papaver alpinum subsp. flaviflorum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
